prerequisites: An active inFlow Inventory subscription on a plan that includes API access.
An API key generated from the inFlow Integrations page (provides the api_key field).
Your inFlow account's Company ID, found on the same Integrations page (provides the companyid field).

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: inFlow authenticates each request with an API key sent in the Authorization header using the Bearer scheme. Provide this value in the connector's api_key field. The companyid field identifies which inFlow account the key acts on and is included in the API request path.

setupSteps.title: Confirm your plan includes API access
setupSteps.body: Ensure you have an active inFlow Inventory subscription on a plan that includes API access. API access is available only for select plans.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: In the inFlow app, open your account settings and go to the Integrations page. Click 'Add new API key', give the key a name, and copy the generated key. Store it securely, as it grants access to your inFlow data.

setupSteps.title: Find your Company ID
setupSteps.body: On the same Integrations page where you created the key, locate your Company ID, the identifier for your inFlow Inventory account.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated API key in the api_key field and your Company ID in the companyid field. The connector sends the key in the Authorization header with the Bearer scheme and uses the Company ID in the request path when calling the inFlow API at https://cloudapi.inflowinventory.com.

sources.title: inFlow Inventory API source docs - dltHub
